MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Sigrid E. Owczarek, appeared for Defendant/moving party
Jeff M. Flacker, appeared for Plaintiff/opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.
The court finds that the motion was timely brought. Plaintiff did not respond by October 12, 2005, negating the agreement that Defendant must bring the motion within two weeks of that date. The basis of the argument is that the court should overlook the obvious typographical error despite the clear intent to obtain the responses within two weeks.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 92-94. The response given to No. 92 is nothing more than a restatement of the question. It does not offer any facts alleged to support a claim of the contention that Plaintiffs had suffered extreme financial hardship. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
